sous_titres.text: Dude, you have to pick up your phone!
Uh, hi. Um, I need help. I need an ambulance.
Uh, no, no, he's not-- he's not responding.
I don't know. I don't know. I just--
I just found him like this. Something is really wrong.
Go get it. Go get it.
What's up, man? What are you looking at?
Who's my bestest friend? You? Is it you?
You're so cute! I could die!
Aww. There's my bestest friend. Look at him. Look at him.
Nope. Stay here, boy. Stay. Sit.
Todd, what the hell is going on?
Oh, yeah. That's why I called.
Um, I decided to get out of town for a bit.
Do you know where Grandpa hid the gate key?
Do you know where he hid the key or not?
There is no way that the doctor said this was okay!
You can't just go wherever the hell you want anymore!
They keys, Vera. Where are the keys?
Exactly. It's available. I'm not bothering anybody.
I needed a break. This could be good for me.
Okay, I need you close in case something goes wrong.
I gotta go. Call me when you calm down.
Wait! Wait, wait! Just tell me that you're oka--
There'll be a lot of cleaning up to do
because of the weather over the next couple of days.
It should help out that at least Thursday
More rain comes in on Friday night...
But it beats the hospital, right?
So, how long have you been planning this?
Planning? No planning. It wasn't some big thing, Vera.
- The red light's on. - I just needed to go.
Hm. No. Yeah. That totally makes sense.
The cursed family house is a great place to relax.
It's not really how I remember it.
You think "house in the woods" sounds so peaceful.
But didn't Grandpa just have a ton of dogs?
Yeah, but they all just kept running away.
Indy, on the other hand, never leaves my side.
I can't poop without a witness.
Eww! Don't tell me that. I don't need to know that.
Grandpa had a ton of these VHS tapes.
Mostly this and shitty horror films.
I think he was taping over them.
What's the point of making a home movie if you live alone?
Can you tell there's something wrong with him?
Not at all. He seems happy.
Well, that's how Grandpa's generation did it, you know?
even when you're rotting from the inside out, right?
I guess living in a haunted house takes your mind off it.
Oh, yeah? No one has lived there for more than a few weeks.
- Grandpa did. - Yeah, and it killed him.
Look, uh... you just have to promise me
that at the first sign of relapse,
you're gonna get in that car and you are coming back.
Okay. Sure. But the rehab promised me...
It's all my family buried under here.
They all died pretty young, too.
What the hell are you doing out here?
I've got traps and snares all over these woods.
Good to see you all grown up.
How long will you be staying with us out here in the boonies?
Well, indefinitely. I gave up my old place.
I just pray for you after everything,
Well, we're gonna make it our own.
Just gotta clear it out, get the electric going.
I got an old generator if you need it.
I'm just down the hill. Come by any time you need help.
Your grandpa was out here with his dog, too.
After we found his body, we never did find that dog.
You're sure you know how to set this up?
Electric for old houses is pretty tough.
Yeah. I got it. Thank you, though.
Just make sure you get it running
Not really safe when it's wet.
Yeah. No problem. Have a good night.
have you noticed Indy acting weird lately?
Like staring at nothing or sniffing for something he can't find.
- One more time. Vera... - This is serious. Okay?
You're the one that keeps telling me
that he won't leave you alone, so I went online and...
Does it seem like he's searching for something
that you can't see or something that doesn't exist?
Oh, you mean being a dog?
Trust me. There's nothing wrong with Indy.
Todd, I'm not worried there's something wrong with Indy.
I'm worried there's something wrong with you.
Okay? They-- They use dogs to detect all kinds of stuff now.
Oh, yeah. I'm aware. Like bombs, cocaine.
Okay. Seriously. Dogs' noses are very sensitive.
- Ah. Jesus Christ. - They can detect all kinds of stuff that tests miss.
And doctors have even been using dogs--
Come on. You silly goose. Huh?
For countless centuries, mankind dwelled in the dark.
we see evidence of the most successful partnership
between any two species on the planet.
With keener senses than their human partners,
dogs protected cave dwellers from unseen dangers
and alerted them to the presence of predators.
Not so fast, boy. Come around here.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
Hey, buddy, buddy, buddy, buddy, buddy.
Who's a handsome man? A big, handsome man!
Hey. Bud. Leave that alone. Come on, Indy.
Alright, Indy. You good? Guard the house.
Can you just go lay down somewhere?
I said give me a minute.
I said get the fuck out of here!
I leave to my daughter, the vegan.
of one family have lived in this place.
Are you still up for me coming out this weekend?
When did we decide on that?
Oh. Well, you know, I just thought that it could be really--
I thought that it would be nice.
You want to check in on me.
I-I'm not trying to nag you. I just...
I really just want to make sure that you're okay.
- You are so full of it. - What?
Can I have one day where I don't have to be a patient?
Can you help me with that? - I'm not trying to make you--
Because whenever you call-- and you call all the time--
I'm just sitting here dreading and waiting
for you to feel like we've chatted enough
that you can ask one of your inane questions.
You know what? Maybe this would be helpful.
Let me save you the trouble of asking
on your next ten phone calls.
No, you're not sorry. Shut up.
That's what I want. Shut up.
Let's make a new rule. Until I come to you
with some kind of crisis, you just lay off.
No. No, no, no, no, no, no, no, no.
I'm sorry. It's just too late.
Maybe if you had come in earlier, before it progressed.
So you're rejecting me because I'm too sick?
Clinical trials have strict entry requirements.
You don't even treat half the people you say you will.
And if it works on me, it'll definitely work, right?
Why are you trying to reject someone
that wants to be your guinea pig?
I'm sorry. It just doesn't work like that.
What are you doing there, Todd?
Do you know if any hyssop grows around here?
Honeysuckle root. I'm collecting those things.
No. Sorry. Never heard of them.
All good. All good. I'll figure it out.
Well, figure it out over there 'cause you're getting
pretty close to where I placed those fox traps.
You take care of yourself, son!
What are you looking at, boy?
How did you get back here?
How did you do this? How did you get up here?
Okay. Come on., Indy. Let's go.
